title: What is Story Bible — Sudowrite Documentation
key_claim: Story Bible holds genre, style, synopsis, characters, worldbuilding, and outline as named fields that the AI silently references during generation.
relevance: Reference for verb-based AI grammar and structured-context pattern — basis for our chapter-generation IA recommendation.

title: Sudowrite Review 2026: Tested w/ 3 Stories — Best AI for Fiction? (Nerdynav)
key_claim: Sudowrite organizes its UX around three named verbs (Write/Describe/Brainstorm) and a Canvas surface for plot/character relationships; users praise the verb-based grammar and find Canvas useful but occasionally disorganized.
relevance: Validates verb-based AI surfacing and shows the limits of unconstrained mind-map/canvas surfaces.

title: Novelcrafter Changelog & Codex UX (feedback.novelcrafter.com)
key_claim: Novelcrafter's UX is built around 'context-at-a-glance', a 3-panel layout (manuscript / story-beats or Codex / inspector), with auto-detection of Codex references in prose and per-scene context selection for AI prompts.
relevance: Direct reference for our scene-editing workspace 3-pane shell and auto-mention detection.

title: Novelcrafter Review: Powerful for Fiction Writers, Frustrating to Set Up (April 2026)
key_claim: Most-cited complaints: BYOK setup feels like 'developer work, not writer work', Codex maintenance is 'exhausting', and detailed outlines often don't reach the model — the platform claims more than the prompt builder uses.
relevance: Anti-pattern catalog: don't gate first-run on API keys; show users exactly what context the AI received.

title: Sudowrite Trustpilot Reviews (UK)
key_claim: Reports of glitches (Beats feature failing while consuming credits), slow support, and Terms-of-Service ambiguity around IP ownership of user inputs.
relevance: Reliability and trust gripes feed our 'show what the AI did and what it cost' principle.

title: NovelAI Lorebook Documentation
key_claim: Lorebook entries are inserted into the AI's context only when their trigger keywords appear in recent text — a hybrid of always-on memory and manual selection.
relevance: Pattern reference for our story-memory workspace: keyword-triggered auto-injection on top of manual selection.

title: NovelAI Review (2026): Great for World-Building, But There's a Catch (Shakespeare AI Blog)
key_claim: Even at Opus-tier 8,192-token context, only ~6–7k recent words are visible; the interface gives no warning when context rolls over, causing sudden 'character amnesia'. Users manually correct narrative flow constantly.
relevance: Anti-pattern: silent context rollover. Drives our 'context warning' recommendation.

title: Integrating Scrivener's Binder, Corkboard, and Outliner — Literature & Latte
key_claim: Binder, Corkboard, and Outliner are three views of the same underlying tree; rearranging in one view is reflected in all three. The Inspector holds metadata (synopsis, keywords, notes) — the corkboard card synopsis IS the Inspector synopsis.
relevance: Canonical reference for the 'same-data, multiple-projections' IA principle — our top recommendation.

title: The Inspector (Scrivener 3's revised user interface) — ScrivenerVirgin
key_claim: The Inspector is the right-hand pane that holds metadata about the currently open document; it survives across editor changes.
relevance: Validates inspector-as-metadata pattern (not duplicated prose).

title: Ulysses Review — Codeless / Selfpublishing.com / Vice
key_claim: Distraction-free first-class modes (typewriter, dark, focus, full-screen). Filters create live folders; library is a first-class IA. Cons: Apple-only, subscription expensive, almost no formatting capability.
relevance: Reference for first-class focus modes and saved-view IA pattern.

title: Plottr Review (Reedsy & Kindlepreneur)
key_claim: Plottr's strength is template-scaffolded story structures (Hero's Journey, Three-Act, Save the Cat) and drag-and-drop timeline cards. Weakness: no writing surface, performance degrades past a card threshold, limited export.
relevance: Reference for template-driven empty states and 'plan view must support inline edit or jump-to-edit'.

title: Obsidian for Fiction Writers — Loreteller / XPress Book Box / Iain F Macleod
key_claim: Obsidian out of the box is not a writing tool; it's a note engine with no opinion. New users link aggressively because the graph 'looks impressive', but meaningful links should connect entities with narrative relationships, otherwise the graph is noise.
relevance: Anti-pattern reference: a freeform graph without curation is not navigable. Drives our typed/filtered atlas recommendation.

title: Why I stopped using Notion — UX Planet & Hacker News thread
key_claim: Notion's flexibility is its weakness: users are overwhelmed by complexity. Adding a single block reveals a long scroll of options. Steep learning curve, decision fatigue.
relevance: Anti-pattern: infinite generality has a UX cost. Validates our 6-fixed-workspaces approach.

title: Sudowrite Pricing & Credit Complaints (CheckThat.ai, costbench.com, feedback.sudowrite.com)
key_claim: Heavy users report 4 chapters / 7–18k words consuming 2M credits; the credit system is 'opaque', costs vary by model and length; users repeatedly request unlimited or roll-over plans.
relevance: Anti-pattern: hidden/variable AI cost. Drives our 'predictable cost telemetry' principle.

title: ShortlyAI / Jasper acquisition & Jasper Trustpilot complaints
key_claim: ShortlyAI was absorbed into Jasper's Boss Mode; Jasper itself has billing complaints (continued billing after cancellation, no use of already-paid days after pause).
relevance: Pricing/trust UX lesson: cancellation and pause UX matters.

title: Plot Bunni — FOSS Novel Writing Suite (plotbunni.com / GitHub)
key_claim: Open-source, browser-based, IndexedDB-local storage, BYOK to OpenRouter/LMStudio/Koboldcpp; 100% free.
relevance: Validates local-first storage and open-source as trust features.

title: Notion Performance & Information Architecture critiques (hackceleration.com, capterra)
key_claim: Performance degrades past 5,000 records (load times 3–5s/page). Users build for imagined future workflows rather than current behavior; IA should reflect cognitive patterns, not data normalization.
relevance: Performance-budget warning for our atlas/knowledge view; IA principle for cognitive-pattern-first design.

title: Logseq vs Roam Research — Travis Chan / Sweet Setup / Saasweep
key_claim: Outliner paradigm has a steep learning curve; many users never reach proficiency. Logseq performance issues past thousands of pages. Roam $15/mo for what Logseq does free.
relevance: Anti-pattern: outliner-everywhere is overkill for fiction; bidirectional links should be sparse/explicit.

title: Where should AI sit in your UI? — UX Collective / Sharang Sharma
key_claim: Maps emerging AI UI patterns: persistent side panel for collaborative work; inline ghost text for short completions; inline action menu at selection for span operations.
relevance: Direct guidance for AI-surfacing decisions across our chapter, scene, branch, and memory workspaces.

title: AI UX Patterns | Inline Action — Shape of AI
key_claim: Inline action menus surface AI verbs (shorten, expand, rephrase, summarize) at the cursor/selection. Lets users scope work at word/sentence/section/block granularity before invoking AI.
relevance: Pattern reference for span-action menu in scene editor.

title: Empty States — UserOnboard / Smashing Magazine / Toptal
key_claim: Empty states are an onboarding opportunity, not chrome. Two parts instruction to one part delight. Best practice is to preload sample data or auto-generate starter content.
relevance: Direct rationale for sample-world / template-prompt empty states across our 6 workspaces.

title: React Router SPA & dynamic tab state preservation (reactrouter.com / GitHub discussions)
key_claim: React Router supports nested layouts perfect for dashboard tabs. Tabs should preserve state when switching; Spotify mobile is the canonical 'route per tab, shared component' pattern.
relevance: Architectural rationale for splitting our App.tsx monolith into a router-driven shell.

title: NovelPad Review — Selfpublishing.com / Comfortable Shoes Studio
key_claim: Cloud SPA. Chapters → scene cards with drag-and-drop reorder & auto-renumber; scene editor pops up over outline (half/full screen); ProWritingAid embedded; autosave every 60 seconds; realtime collab.
relevance: Reference for 'jump-to-edit' from card view and autosave cadence.

title: Unlock Your Writing Potential with NovelAI's Memory and Lorebooks — Eightify
key_claim: NovelAI exposes the AI's working memory: current text window, active lorebook entries, author's note, memory injections. This transparency is something other tools lack.
relevance: Pattern reference for our 'context inspector' pill — strongest AI-transparency pattern in survey.

title: Dabble Plot Grid — Help Center / Reedsy / Kindlepreneur
key_claim: Plot Grid is columns (Plot Lines) × rows (Scenes/Chapters) × cards (Plot Points), with drag-and-drop. Inspired by Harry Potter plotting method. Cleanest visual cross-cutting subplot tracker.
relevance: Reference for branch/timeline workspace: cross-cutting subplot view.

title: Aeon Timeline & Campfire — Mythic Scribes / Reedsy / Kindlepreneur
key_claim: Aeon Timeline combines timeline + spreadsheet + mindmap + 'narrative view' (drag chronological events into non-chronological reading order). Campfire offers 17 modules sold individually (Characters, Maps, Magic, Languages...).
relevance: Narrative-view pattern (chronological vs. reading order projection) directly applicable to our branch/timeline workspace.

title: Make Canvas work more like a mind-map — Sudowrite Feedback
key_claim: Users publicly request that Canvas behave more like a true mind-map, allowing connections between character cards and plot lines, with the ability to generate parallel and converging plotlines.
relevance: Validates that unconstrained 2D canvases need stronger structural opinions to feel useful.

title: Novelcrafter Codex Documentation
key_claim: Codex auto-detects references in text; clicking shows a preview popover; prompts auto-pull referenced entries into context. Per-scene curation of which entries the model sees.
relevance: Reference for auto-mention detection and per-scene context selection in our scene workspace.

title: Scrivener Learning Curve Forums — Literature & Latte / Quora / Kindlepreneur
key_claim: Scrivener's UI is described as a '747 cockpit', a 'patchwork of basic functionality with miscellaneous add-ons', no consistent UI between components. Compile/export is universally hated.
relevance: Anti-pattern: visual-consistency drift across panels. Validates need for shared shell across our 6 workspaces.

title: Best Novel Writing Software Compared (Chapter Blog / Legendfiction Substack)
key_claim: Reviewer of 22 writing tools describes Novelcrafter as feeling 'a little dense, like there's just a bit too much going on visually', and Squibler as 'bulky and overbuilt for most writers'.
relevance: Anti-pattern: visual density. Drives our 'density knob' recommendation in card layouts.

title: World Anvil Review — Kindlepreneur / Self-Publishing School
key_claim: World Anvil's strength is template-driven articles with embedded prompts ('describe the climate', 'describe daily life'). Weakness: extensive functionality is overwhelming, steep learning curve, hundreds of optional modules.
relevance: Reference for embedded-prompt empty states in our world-building workspace; warning about module bloat.

title: Worldwand — Premium Worldbuilding for Writers and GMs
key_claim: Markets explicitly as 'a three-panel workspace built for creation and recall — browse structure on one side, edit in the center, and inspect detail, relationships, pending AI changes, or chat context on the other'.
relevance: Confirms three-pane is the default expected layout for premium writer/GM tools in 2026.

title: Designing Trustworthy AI Assistants: 9 Simple UX Patterns — Orange Loops
key_claim: Trustworthy AI assistants need granular feedback (thumbs up/down per response, optional tags 'inaccurate', 'irrelevant'), display insights without overwhelming, and offer directional follow-ups (zoom in vs. zoom out).
relevance: Pattern reference for AI critique/feedback surfacing without overwhelm.

title: Three-Pane Layout in Editor / IDE Tools — Firefox DevTools 3-pane mode docs / Polypane Workspaces
key_claim: The three-pane layout (browse | edit | inspect) is the dominant pattern across editor IDEs (VSCode, Firefox DevTools, Polypane) — preserves three persistent contexts: where you are, what you're working on, and what's true about it.
relevance: Architectural rationale for our shell.

title: AI UX Patterns | Regenerate — Shape of AI
key_claim: Regenerate has two design modes: overwrite (lose previous) vs branching (each retry is a separate version). For creative work, branching with cyclable history is the strong pattern; overwrite-only is widely disliked.
relevance: Direct reference for branch/timeline workspace: regenerate = branch, not overwrite.

title: Autosave Patterns — UI-Patterns.com / Pajamas (GitLab) / Primer (GitHub)
key_claim: Autosave UX: show 'Saving…' (with spinner) or 'Saved' + relative timestamp. For long-form, prefer 'Draft saved 1 min ago'. Don't remove the Save button — users panic without one.
relevance: Direct guidance for our autosave & draft-vs-saved pattern.

title: Plot Bunni GitHub README (MangoLion/plotbunni)
key_claim: FOSS Novel Writing Suite with AI assistance, IndexedDB local storage, no accounts, no telemetry; works offline.
relevance: Reference architecture for fully local-first AI writing app.

title: Don't Prioritize Efficiency Over Expectations — Nielsen Norman Group
key_claim: When implementing autosave, users still expect (and reach for) a Save action — meeting that expectation matters more than removing a redundant click.
relevance: NN/G principle backing the 'keep the Save button' recommendation.

title: Sudowrite Story Bible Even More Features changelog
key_claim: Story Bible has been iterated heavily since launch — adding new fields, character templates, and worldbuilding sub-sections — showing it's the most-actively-evolved part of Sudowrite.
relevance: Indicates that structured-context store is a long-term competitive moat in this category.

title: Information Architecture Articles (NN/G topic page)
key_claim: NN/G's IA research emphasizes mental models, navigation hierarchy, and 'cognitive walkthrough' as the core methods for designing complex tool IA.
relevance: Methodological backing for our 5 IA principles synthesis.

title: Advanced React Component Patterns
key_claim: Establishes compound components, render props, state reducers, and control props as the canonical decomposition vocabulary used by Radix, Headless UI, and modern libraries.
relevance: Authoritative source for refactoring a god component via compound-component / hooks-based separation.

title: React Hooks: Compound Components
key_claim: Compound components share implicit state via context — the standard pattern for accessible widget composition.
relevance: Pattern to replace nested prop-drilled JSX in App.tsx workspaces.

title: Testing Implementation Details
key_claim: Tests on implementation details break on refactor and fail to fail on regressions; query the way users do.
relevance: Foundational for the Vitest+RTL test suite during a strangler-fig refactor.

title: Introducing the react-testing-library
key_claim: RTL queries the DOM the way the user would (label text, role, text); avoid testing internal state.
relevance: Drives the rewrite of any wrapper.state()-style tests.

title: How to know what to test
key_claim: Test prioritized user flows, not coverage percentages.
relevance: Helps choose what tests survive the strangler-fig migration.

title: Taming the God Component: A Framework-Agnostic Guide to the Component Responsibility Score (CRS)
key_claim: CRS thresholds: <50 healthy, 50-100 monitor, >100 refactor alert; provides a quantitative refactor heuristic.
relevance: Direct quantitative justification for breaking up a 1659-line App.tsx.

title: React Anti-Patterns and Best Practices - Do's and Don'ts
key_claim: Replace bloated components with multiple useStates by useReducer or extracted hooks.
relevance: Direct guidance for the 30+ useState case in App.tsx.

title: Common Sense Refactoring of a Messy React Component
key_claim: Break component into smaller pieces; replace handler logic with library calls; aim for sub-250-LOC files.
relevance: Concrete heuristics matching the audited file's symptoms.

title: Container/Presentational Pattern
key_claim: Modern container layer is often a custom hook; presentational components remain pure props-to-UI.
relevance: Maps directly to the recommended hook-based extraction strategy.

title: React Stack Patterns 2026
key_claim: For smaller, internal, or highly custom SPAs, use Vite + libraries instead of frameworks.
relevance: Direct endorsement of the audited app's architectural choice over Next/Remix.

title: Presentational vs Container Components: Still Relevant in 2025?
key_claim: Pattern remains a guidepost; modern React encourages flexible composition via hooks and colocated logic.
relevance: Validates hook-based 'container' approach for 2025/2026.

title: React Libraries for 2025
key_claim: Zustand is becoming the de facto standard; URL state, server cache, and Server Components reduce client-state need.
relevance: Strongest single-source consensus check for state management defaults.

title: React Folder Structure in 5 Steps [2025]
key_claim: Feature-based folders + colocation; layer-folder structure breaks down beyond ~15-20 components.
relevance: Guides the proposed src/features/<workspace>/ structure.

title: Local Storage in React
key_claim: localStorage is appropriate for UI/layout state; avoid for sensitive data.
relevance: Backs the recommendation to persist multi-pane layout in localStorage.

title: Why You Should Use Redux in 2024
key_claim: Redux maintainer can't unconditionally recommend Redux; pick it for enforced patterns + middleware ecosystem in large teams.
relevance: Discharges Redux for our single-team app.

title: Getting Started — Redux Toolkit
key_claim: RTK is the official recommended Redux approach; eliminates immutable-update bugs.
relevance: Reference for the 'when Redux is the answer' branch of the decision matrix.

title: Choosing a State Management Library for React in 2026
key_claim: Zustand pragmatic default; Jotai for atomic state; RTK for enforced patterns; signals not yet consensus.
relevance: 2026 cross-comparison reference.

title: Top 5 React State Management Tools Developers Actually Use in 2026 and Why
key_claim: Zustand at ~20M weekly downloads, ~1.2KB; most popular choice in 2026.
relevance: Adoption-data backing for Zustand default.

title: Zustand vs Redux Toolkit vs Jotai
key_claim: Zustand subscription-based, Jotai atom-level, RTK selector-based; performance ordering Zustand>Jotai>RTK on a single update.
relevance: Performance comparison for the decision matrix.

title: Comparison — Jotai docs
key_claim: Jotai consists of primitive atoms; best for derived-heavy state.
relevance: Sets the niche for Jotai in the decision matrix.

title: React State Management in 2025: What You Actually Need
key_claim: Most state should live in URL or server cache; client state libraries are last resort.
relevance: Validates the URL-routable workspaces recommendation.

title: React patterns to avoid common pitfalls in local state management
key_claim: Concrete failure modes of useState overuse: redundant state, stale closures, re-render storms.
relevance: Diagnoses the 30+ useState symptom directly.

title: useState & Redundant State Anti-Pattern
key_claim: Storing computed values as state opens up bugs because contributors forget to keep them in sync.
relevance: Names the redundant-state anti-pattern.

title: Choosing the State Structure (React docs)
key_claim: Unify state variables that always change together; avoid contradictions in state; avoid redundant/derivable state.
relevance: Authoritative React-team guidance.

title: Optimistic Updates — TanStack Query Docs
key_claim: Two patterns: onMutate cache update, or use returned variables from useMutation; supports rollback on failure.
relevance: Critical for AI-app mutation rollback.

title: React Query vs TanStack Query vs SWR: A 2025 Comparison
key_claim: TanStack Query has fuller mutation system + devtools; SWR is smaller (5.3KB vs 16.2KB) but lighter on features.
relevance: Justifies TanStack Query over SWR for AI app.

title: Why Senior Developers Choose TanStack Query Over Fetch and useEffect
key_claim: TanStack Query eliminates ~50% of useState/useEffect data-fetching boilerplate; built-in cache, refetch, dedup.
relevance: Quantifies the boilerplate-reduction win.

title: React Query vs useEffect: 2026 Data Fetching Best Practices
key_claim: useEffect leads to network waterfalls, race conditions, and zero caching; RQ is 'professional standard'.
relevance: 2026 framing of the server-state question.

title: React Hook Form vs Formik 2026: Best Form Library for Production Apps
key_claim: RHF + Zod is the 2026 industry standard for greenfield projects; Formik unmaintained.
relevance: Picks RHF for the decision matrix.

title: React Hook Form vs Formik: A technical and performance comparison
key_claim: RHF uses uncontrolled inputs/refs to minimize re-renders; ~9KB zero-deps vs Formik 13KB+deps.
relevance: Performance evidence for RHF.

title: TanStack Router & TanStack Start vs Next.js vs React Router / Remix
key_claim: TanStack Router's 100% type-safe routing; URL→state→data→UI design.
relevance: Primary source for TanStack Router decision.

title: TanStack Router vs React Router v7: Type-Safe Routing in 2026
key_claim: RR v7 advanced features primarily live in framework mode; TanStack better for pure SPAs needing type safety.
relevance: Direct head-to-head for our routing decision.

title: TanStack Router vs React Router
key_claim: TanStack Router devtools render in-app showing route tree, search params, loader state, pending navigations.
relevance: DX advantage for AI-heavy apps with rich URL params.

title: wouter — minimalist routing for React and Preact
key_claim: ~2.2KB router with hook-first API; zero deps.
relevance: Niche option if bundle is paramount; otherwise too thin for our 6-workspace app.

title: An introduction to Wouter: A React Router alternative
key_claim: 1.3KB; three API styles (hooks, low-level, components).
relevance: Comparison data for the routing table.

title: Respecting Browser Navigation in Single Page Applications
key_claim: SPAs naive of URL changes break deep linking and refresh; History API is the bridge.
relevance: Foundational case for adding a router.

title: Stanford CS142 Lecture Notes — Single Page Applications
key_claim: Client-side routing maps URL to component; History API enables back/forward.
relevance: Authoritative academic framing of SPA routing.

title: Remix vs Next.js (Remix blog)
key_claim: Remix bundles ~30% smaller; data mutation flow simpler.
relevance: Comparator for the framework migration question.

title: Top 10 Full Stack Frameworks in 2026: Next.js, Remix, Nuxt, SvelteKit, and More
key_claim: RR7 SPA mode best for dashboards/admin/internal tools; Next.js LCP advantage less material when SEO is moot.
relevance: Direct support for staying SPA on Vite.

title: The State of CSS-in-JS in 2026: Is It Dead?
key_claim: Runtime CSS-in-JS dead for new projects (RSC incompat, bundle, blocking render); Tailwind +100% in 3 years.
relevance: Key 2026 styling-consensus citation.

title: Tailwind CSS vs. Vanilla CSS — A Pragmatic Comparison for 2026
key_claim: Tailwind production CSS 5-20KB regardless of project size; growth logarithmic.
relevance: Bundle-size argument for Tailwind.

title: Tailwind vs CSS Modules (2026)
key_claim: Mixed approach: Tailwind for layout, CSS Modules for complex components, custom properties for theming.
relevance: Justifies the hybrid styling recommendation.

title: React UI libraries in 2025: Comparing shadcn/ui, Radix, Mantine, MUI, Chakra & more
key_claim: shadcn/ui is not a package — CLI scaffolds Radix-based Tailwind components into your repo.
relevance: Defines shadcn for the audited team.

title: shadcn/ui vs Base UI vs Radix: Components in 2026
key_claim: shadcn/ui supports both Radix and Base UI primitives; February 2026 Visual Builder reduces setup further.
relevance: Latest shadcn capability snapshot.

title: Best React UI Libraries (2026): MUI vs Chakra vs Shadcn vs Radix
key_claim: Mantine emerged as compelling MUI/Chakra alternative; CSS Modules-based, smaller bundles.
relevance: Mantine fallback option.

title: How to Migrate Your Existing CSS to TailwindCSS
key_claim: Disable preflight, add prefix, migrate file-by-file; coexist multiple stylesheets temporarily.
relevance: Operational migration plan.

title: Migrating from Bootstrap to Tailwind
key_claim: Migrate biggest breakpoint first; verify in browser per file before committing.
relevance: Gives a specific, repeatable workflow.

title: Dockview — Docking Layout Manager
key_claim: Zero-dep docking layout for IDE-like UIs; tabs, groups, grids, splitviews, drag-drop, floating panels, popout windows; portions inspired by VS Code source.
relevance: Heavyweight option if true IDE features needed.

title: react-resizable-panels (bvaughn)
key_claim: autoSaveId persists layouts to localStorage by default; conditional panels persisted per visible-combination; custom storage prop supported.
relevance: Drop-in solution for the multi-pane requirement.

title: Allotment: a React component for resizable split views
key_claim: Allotment is inspired by VS Code's split pane behavior.
relevance: Lightweight resizable-split alternative.

title: Essential tools for implementing React panel layouts
key_claim: Survey of react-resizable-panels, Allotment, Dockview, react-dock for IDE-style layouts.
relevance: Library landscape source.

title: Persisting React State in localStorage
key_claim: Custom hook pattern for safe localStorage state; SSR pitfalls; serialization concerns.
relevance: Layout persistence implementation.

title: Bulletproof React — Project Structure
key_claim: Most code lives in features/; no cross-feature imports; per-feature public API; avoid barrel files (Vite tree-shaking).
relevance: Canonical feature-folder spec.

title: Bulletproof React — Project Standards
key_claim: Absolute imports + ESLint enforcement; layered import boundaries.
relevance: Lint-enforceable rules for the migration.

title: Screaming Architecture — Evolution of a React folder structure
key_claim: Top-level folders should scream the product domain (workspace names) not technical layers.
relevance: Justifies workspace-named feature folders.

title: Screaming Architecture & Colocation: Let Your Project Structure Tell the Story
key_claim: Combine screaming architecture with colocation — files that change together live together.
relevance: Practical heuristic for the new folder layout.

title: The Best React.js Architecture for 2026: Domain-Driven + Feature-Sliced Design
key_claim: Modern React architecture is about clear ownership, domain boundaries, and data contracts — not where files live.
relevance: 2026 framing of the architecture problem.

title: Vite — Backend Integration
key_claim: Vite is recommended in middleware mode when integrating with backends; configureServer adds custom middlewares.
relevance: Defines the embedded-backend pattern in the audited app.

title: Vite — Server-Side Rendering
key_claim: Recommend Vite in middleware mode for SSR but extract from production server.
relevance: Confirms the dev-only nature of Vite middleware.

title: Custom express middleware support — Vite GitHub discussion 6562
key_claim: Vite supports custom middleware via configureServer; production hosting recommends separate backend.
relevance: Direct evidence on middleware mode tradeoffs.

title: Express vs Hono in 2026: Legacy vs Modern Node.js
key_claim: Hono is the pragmatic default in 2026 for new JS APIs; Web Standards APIs, ~14KB, multi-runtime.
relevance: Picks Hono for the backend extraction.

title: Hono vs ElysiaJS vs Nitro (2026)
key_claim: Hono universal across runtimes; Elysia tied to Bun; Nitro tied to Nitro deployments.
relevance: Disambiguates Hono from look-alikes.

title: Best Hono Alternatives for TypeScript Backend Development in 2026
key_claim: When you need DBs, services, jobs, observability, Hono needs glue; alternatives like Encore.ts or NestJS provide structure.
relevance: Sets the limit of Hono for our backend.

title: hono-trpc example
key_claim: Hono + tRPC v10 worked example on a separate backend port.
relevance: Reference impl for the recommended split.

title: Vitest — expect API
key_claim: expect.poll retries assertions; must be awaited (Vitest 3+); not for snapshot/.resolves/.rejects/toThrow.
relevance: Drives async UI testing strategy.

title: Vitest — Testing Asynchronous Code
key_claim: Promises, polling, and timeouts patterns in Vitest.
relevance: Guide for streaming AI test patterns.

title: Vitest — Component Testing
key_claim: expect.element auto-retries with helpful diagnostics; preferred over expect.poll for DOM presence.
relevance: Recommendation for browser-mode async assertions.

title: react-hooks-testing-library
key_claim: Use renderHook only for reusable hooks/libraries; otherwise test the component using the hook.
relevance: Drives the hook-testing strategy.

title: @testing-library/react
key_claim: Encourage user-perspective testing; query by role/label/text.
relevance: Canonical API reference.

title: Refactoring Legacy Code with the Strangler Fig Pattern
key_claim: Strangler Fig enables modular rollout, easy rollback, localized testing, isolated risk.
relevance: Industrial-scale validation for the migration approach.

title: Strangler fig pattern — AWS Prescriptive Guidance
key_claim: Incremental migration; anti-corruption layer routes to new services until old is fully replaced.
relevance: Architectural reference for the multi-PR migration.

title: The Strangler Fig Pattern and Microfrontends
key_claim: Apply Strangler Fig on the frontend by injecting React Islands into legacy SPAs.
relevance: Frontend-specific Strangler Fig application.

title: State of React 2025
key_claim: TanStack Query usage broadly accepted; Zustand top of state-management satisfaction.
relevance: Community-wide signal.

title: Do You Need State Management in 2025? React Context vs Zustand vs Jotai vs Redux
key_claim: Pick by complexity; Context for prop-drilling, Zustand for medium/large apps, Jotai for atomic state, Redux for enterprise.
relevance: Approachable summary for decision matrix.

title: Persisting React State in LocalStorage
key_claim: useState + useEffect pattern, custom hook abstraction; serialization with JSON.stringify.
relevance: Reference for layout-state persistence implementation.

title: React Hook Form vs Formik: The Good, Bad, and Ugly
key_claim: Formik repo dormant; RHF actively maintained; uncontrolled vs controlled is the key difference.
relevance: Maintenance status for forms decision.

title: Revisiting URL-Based State Management in SPAs
key_claim: URL is the simplest, most shareable state store for filters/searches/sorts.
relevance: Argument for routable workspaces with searchable params.

title: SPA Routing — Dorset Tech Glossary
key_claim: SPA routing intercepts URL changes and renders components dynamically without page reload.
relevance: Definitional source for routing recommendation.

title: How we redesigned the Linear UI (part II)
key_claim: Linear's 2024 refresh dimmed the sidebar so the main content takes precedence, made tabs more compact (rounded, smaller icons/text) instead of full-width, and used view headers + side panels to surface filters/meta properties without crowding the canvas.
relevance: Direct template for our left rail + content + right meta panel; shows how to reduce visual weight of nav so writing canvas dominates.

title: Linear Changelog — Collapsible Sidebar
key_claim: Sidebar can collapse to give the canvas full width on demand; navigation becomes a peek/hover surface.
relevance: Justifies a togglable rail (Cmd+\) for our Writing workspace where prose density matters most.

title: Linear Concepts (Workspace, Teams, Views)
key_claim: A Linear workspace is a single container; views are dynamic filtered slices of one underlying graph rather than separate apps.
relevance: Maps onto our problem: World/Memory/Atlas should be views over one underlying entity graph, not three separate workspaces.

title: Linear — Design for the AI age
key_claim: AI in Linear is summoned via command palette / inline context, not pinned chrome; the product avoids 'AI everywhere' chrome drift.
relevance: Counterpoint for the 'AI everywhere' anti-pattern; supports putting Simulation/Runtime AI behind palette + inline triggers, not always-visible panels.

title: Linear design: the SaaS design trend that's boring and bettering UI
key_claim: Linear's 2025 monochrome palette and reduced color have become a copied baseline; saturated color is reserved for state, not chrome.
relevance: Argues for our chrome being neutral/monochrome and reserving saturated colors for in-prose semantics (sim events, world entities).

title: VS Code — User Interface
key_claim: VS Code's six regions: Activity Bar, Primary Side Bar, Editor Group(s), Panel, Secondary Side Bar, Status Bar. Activity Bar is the persistent mode switch; sidebars host views; editor groups own tabs.
relevance: Canonical 'verbs (activity bar) + nouns (sidebar views) + content (editor)' decomposition we should adopt for our 6 workspaces.

title: VS Code — Custom Layout
key_claim: Users can drag/move views between Primary/Secondary sidebar and Panel regions; layout is remembered per workbench.
relevance: Supports moveable, persistable panels rather than hard-coded conditional rendering.

title: VS Code Extension API — UX Guidelines: Sidebars
key_claim: Primary side bar = navigation/structure; Secondary side bar = ancillary opposite-side context; both can host arbitrary views.
relevance: Two-rail asymmetric layout (structure left, context right) is the field-tested pattern for IDE-style workbenches.

title: Cursor Forum — Flexible Panel Layout request
key_claim: Cursor's fixed three-pane (sidebar / editor / AI chat) layout draws repeated user complaints; users want movable AI panel position.
relevance: Cautionary tale: even successful AI-IDE products get pushback when the right-hand AI rail is locked. Bake in panel mobility.

title: Cursor Forum — Customizable AI Panel Layout Position
key_claim: Users explicitly ask for the AI panel to be movable / dockable; rigid right-only AI panels feel cramped on small screens.
relevance: For our Simulation + Runtime panels, allow docking left/right/bottom and a 'pop out' Composition Mode.

title: Notion — New sidebar design will help Notion scale
key_claim: Notion split sidebar into Workspace / Teamspaces / Private sections; teamspaces act as the broadest categorization, hierarchically nesting pages below.
relevance: Supports a hierarchical 'project > book > chapter > scene' sidebar over flat tab switching for Writing workspace.

title: UI Breakdown of Notion's Sidebar (Medium)
key_claim: Notion sidebar is 224px wide on an 8px grid; uses accordion drill-down; right-side line nav doubles as in-page TOC.
relevance: Concrete grid + width numbers for our left rail; argues for adding an in-doc outline to the writing canvas.

title: Design Critique: Notion — Autonomy, Cleanness, Organization
key_claim: Notion's effectiveness comes from progressive disclosure: blocks, slash commands, and toggle headings hide complexity until summoned.
relevance: Justifies / commands inside the writing surface to expose Simulation/World features without permanent chrome.

title: Obsidian Graph view documentation
key_claim: Obsidian distinguishes global graph (whole vault) from local graph (current note's neighborhood), each with filter sidebar.
relevance: Direct precedent for our Atlas: we should ship a 'local atlas' (entities mentioned in current scene) before a 'global atlas'.

title: Obsidian Forum — Open local graph in right sidebar
key_claim: Power users mount the local graph as a right-sidebar pane next to their note, so the graph stays in peripheral vision while writing.
relevance: Pattern for keeping Atlas/World context visible without taking the writing screen real estate.

title: Figma — Design, prototype, and explore layer properties in the right sidebar
key_claim: Right sidebar is contextual: Design / Prototype / Inspect. Content swaps based on selection. Empty selection collapses the panel.
relevance: Inspector pattern: panel content should be contextual to selection, and should self-collapse when nothing is selected (preventing 'database wall' problem).

title: Figma — View layers and pages in the left sidebar
key_claim: Left sidebar = structure (pages, layers). Two-tab toggle (Layers / Assets). Mirrors the IDE pattern of structure-left.
relevance: Reinforces that structure-left / context-right is the dominant creative-tool pattern, not 6 sibling top-level workspaces.

title: Figma Forum — Option to hide the right panel
key_claim: Even Figma users repeatedly ask to fully hide the right panel for canvas focus; current behavior auto-collapses but minimum width still bothers users.
relevance: Confirms inspectors must be hidable, not merely 'minimize-able', for focus-mode work.

title: Sudowrite vs Novelcrafter — Canvas overview
key_claim: Sudowrite Canvas is a freeform card board for character ideas / plot / prose; supports Hero's Journey / Save the Cat / 3-Act / Romancing the Beat overlays. Novelcrafter is panel-dense (manuscript + Codex side-by-side).
relevance: Two valid templates for fiction tools: Sudowrite = uncluttered + AI-summon, Novelcrafter = context-at-a-glance dense panels. Our app currently leans Novelcrafter-dense without the discipline; we should pick a stance.

title: Novelcrafter — Your Novel Writing Software
key_claim: Novelcrafter explicitly markets a 'Codex' (worldbuilding entities) panel that travels with the manuscript; structure-left, manuscript-center, codex-right.
relevance: Strongest direct analog to our World/Memory functions. Confirms structure/manuscript/codex three-pane is a workable IA for fiction with worldbuilding.

title: Literature & Latte — Write Without Distractions Using Composition Mode
key_claim: Composition Mode hides chrome; only a thin Control Bar appears on cursor proximity. Typewriter Scrolling pins the active line vertically centered.
relevance: Template for a 'Writing Focus' mode toggled with one shortcut, removing all 6 workspaces from view temporarily.

title: Skyler Benson — How to Use Scrivener Typewriter Mode
key_claim: Typewriter Scrolling reduces the head-tilt fatigue of writing toward the bottom of the screen by keeping the cursor mid-screen.
relevance: Concrete distraction-reduction interaction we can implement in our Writing surface independent of mode toggles.

title: Kindlepreneur — Scrivener vs Ulysses
key_claim: Ulysses ships dark mode + minimal mode + full-screen + typewriter mode as toggleable single-purpose focus tools.
relevance: Argues we should not bundle everything in one 'focus mode' but offer composable focus toggles.

title: NN/G — 10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design
key_claim: Updated Jan 2024. Heuristic 6 (Recognition rather than recall): show options instead of forcing memory. Heuristic 8 (Aesthetic and minimalist): every extra element competes with relevant content. Heuristic 9 (Help users recover from errors): clear, plain-language error states.
relevance: Direct grounding for command palette (recall→recognition), reducing chrome in 1659-line shell (minimalist), and replacing JSON dumps with friendly errors.

title: NN/G — 10 Heuristics Applied to Complex Applications
key_claim: Complex tools (IDE-class) should use progressive disclosure plus consistent meta-pattern (left structure, center work, right context) to keep heuristics intact under feature load.
relevance: Most direct guidance for our 6-workspace problem; supports collapsing 6 into a stable 3-region workbench.

title: NN/G — Progressive Disclosure
key_claim: Defer secondary options to a subsidiary screen; show primary options by default. Improves task-completion speed 30-50% while preserving feature discoverability ~70-90%.
relevance: Foundational source for hiding Simulation/Runtime advanced controls behind 'Advanced' disclosure / palette commands.

title: NN/G — Information Foraging: a theory of how people navigate the web
key_claim: Users follow 'information scent': trigger words in labels, headings, link text. Specific labels reduce navigation time 30-50% vs generic.
relevance: Renaming the 6 generic workspace names ('World', 'Memory', 'Atlas') to action/content labels (e.g. 'Codex', 'Lore', 'Map') strengthens scent.

title: UX Tigers — Information Scent: How Users Decide Where to Click
key_claim: Trigger words in nav labels make-or-break clickthrough; vague labels push users to search.
relevance: If our left rail says 'World' and 'Memory' and 'Atlas', users can't predict the difference. Concrete fix candidate.

title: NN/G — Scrolling and Attention
key_claim: 57% of viewing time is spent above the fold; 74% in first two screenfuls. First impression formed in <0.05s.
relevance: Argues that whatever we put in the first viewport of the Writing workspace IS the product perception. Currently competing toolbars hurt this.

title: Butterick's Practical Typography — Line Length
key_claim: Average line length should be 45–90 characters including spaces.
relevance: Sets the max-width on the prose column in our Writing workspace; 65ch is a strong default.

title: Butterick's Practical Typography — Summary of Key Rules
key_claim: Body text: 10–12pt print / 15–25px web. Line spacing 120–145% of size. Avoid system defaults (Times/Arial).
relevance: Concrete numeric grounding for our prose CSS.

title: Typotheque — Typesetting principles of Chinese, Japanese, and Korean (CJK) text
key_claim: CJK characters fill the em-box; ~170% line-height is needed to avoid cramped strokes. CJK kerning between punctuation improves readability.
relevance: Critical for Bagua / 八卦 fiction app: leading should be 1.7 for prose body, not 1.4-1.5 default.

title: Asian Absolute — CJK Typesetting in 2025
key_claim: WCAG limit for CJK text is 40 characters per line vs 80 for Latin; 80 Chinese characters becomes 'too dense and exhausting'.
relevance: Our prose column max-width must be expressed in CJK character count (~36-40ch CJK), not 65ch Latin.

title: AZ-Loc — Best Chinese Fonts for Websites (2026)
key_claim: Source Han Serif / Noto Serif CJK SC for prose body; Source Han Sans / PingFang / Misans for UI chrome. Differentiating prose vs UI font is essential.
relevance: Directly informs our typography stack: serif for manuscript text, sans for UI chrome.

title: UXPin — Optimal Line Length for Readability (2026)
key_claim: Latin: 50–75 chars/line ideal, 45–90 acceptable; CJK shorter; lines longer than 90 hurt eye-tracking returns.
relevance: Confirms our hard cap should be 90ch even in low-density mode.

title: iA — feed (Web design is 95% typography / 100E2R)
key_claim: 100E2R = 100% Easy to Read. Body fonts 16px+; macro-typography (line length, leading) matters more than font choice.
relevance: Mentor-source for prose-first IA: typography drives the writing experience, not chrome.

title: SAP Fiori — Content Density (Cozy and Compact)
key_claim: Cozy = touch / casual; Compact = power-user / data-dense. Provide a user toggle, never auto-pick.
relevance: Argue for a Comfortable / Compact toggle so reviewers/editors can switch from prose-comfort to outline-density.

title: Cloudscape Design System — Content Density
key_claim: Compact density not fully applied to read-oriented surfaces because it hurts reading; long-form reading should default to comfortable density.
relevance: Default state for our Writing workspace = comfortable; Atlas/Memory tables can lean compact.

title: Matt Ström-Awn — UI Density
key_claim: Density is about information per unit of attention, not pixels. Linear's density feels low because chrome is muted, not because it's spacious.
relevance: Reframes our 'cluttered' problem: it's high attentional density, not high pixel density. Fix is hierarchy, not whitespace.

title: Philip Davis — Command Palette Interfaces
key_claim: Cmd+K palettes are now table-stakes in modern SaaS (Linear / Figma / Notion / Vercel / Raycast). Best practice: fuzzy search, scoped commands, recents, keyboard-only operation.
relevance: Justifies adding ⌘K palette as first-class navigation alongside the left rail.

title: commandpalette.org — Command palette resources
key_claim: Catalog of Cmd+K patterns across creative + dev tools; converging on identical shortcut and grouping conventions.
relevance: Convention library: our palette should mirror these so users transfer muscle memory in.

title: LogRocket — Tabbed navigation in UX: Where and when to use it
key_claim: Tabs work for parallel siblings of equal weight; fail when content is sequential, when there are >7 tabs, or when there's strong hierarchy.
relevance: Our 6 workspaces are NOT equal weight; Writing is primary, others are support. Tabs are the wrong metaphor.

title: NN/G — Tabs, Used Right
key_claim: Tabs work when items are mutually exclusive, parallel, and limited (~5-7). Bad tabs increase cognitive load; users miss content behind unselected tabs ~60% of the time.
relevance: If we keep mode-switching at all, total mode count should stay ≤5 and modes should be co-equal.

title: UserOnboard — Empty States
key_claim: Empty states are first-impression real-estate; should explain the screen's purpose AND propose the first action.
relevance: Our 6 workspaces almost certainly have weak empty states; opportunity to teach the product surface-by-surface.

title: Smashing Magazine — The Role of Empty States in User Onboarding
key_claim: Best empty states blend illustration + plain-language purpose + one primary CTA. Avoid only icon + 'No data'.
relevance: Concrete pattern for empty Memory / Atlas / Simulation views.

title: Ahmad Alfy — Your URL Is Your State
key_claim: If your SPA forgets state on refresh, you've broken a fundamental web contract. URL is the canonical store for shareable state.
relevance: Our app uses conditional rendering with no URL state — every reload bounces users to default workspace. Highest-priority fix.

title: LogRocket — Advanced React state management using URL parameters
key_claim: URL params + React Router + nested routes give shareable, refresh-safe workspace state with minimal code.
relevance: Implementation reference: replace 1659-line conditional shell with route-driven layout.

title: Wikipedia — Feature creep
key_claim: Feature creep causes software bloat, schedule overruns, and reduced usability. Mitigation: separate functionality; expose extras only via opt-in submenus.
relevance: Frames the 6-workspace symptom: the cure is hierarchy + opt-in, not a 7th workspace.

title: UserJot — Feature Creep: Why Your Product Keeps Getting Worse
key_claim: Personal/solo-built tools especially fall to feature creep because there's no PM gate. Symptom: top-level nav growth.
relevance: Specific to our context: solo-built fiction app with growing top-level surface area.

title: LogRocket — Overusing AI is ruining UX
key_claim: Constant AI suggestions are exhausting; in creative fields users want assistive AI, not always-on AI chrome.
relevance: Anti-pattern fit: Simulation/Runtime panels always-visible = AI fatigue. Move to summon-on-demand.

title: UX Tigers — Hello AI Agents: Goodbye UI Design
key_claim: AI in creative tools should be inline / contextual; persistent AI chrome competes with the user's own thought.
relevance: Reinforces summon-on-demand for our AI features.

title: Idea Theorem — AI Design Patterns for Next-Gen UX (2025)
key_claim: Emerging patterns: inline copilot ribbon, side drawer, command-palette AI, ghost-text suggestions. Persistent right-rail AI is on the decline.
relevance: Modern menu of options to choose from, replacing one big right-side AI panel.

title: Logseq — How to work with the right-hand sidebar
key_claim: Right sidebar holds stacked references / queries / linked-mentions; you can pin multiple panes vertically.
relevance: Pattern for stacking 'Memory' (recent context) above 'Atlas' (entity hover) in one rail rather than separate workspaces.

title: The Sweet Setup — Obsidian vs Roam vs Logseq
key_claim: All three PKM tools converged on left-rail (vault/structure) + main pane (note) + right-rail (back-references/graph). Differences are stylistic, not structural.
relevance: Our 'World/Memory/Atlas' merger should follow this convergent pattern.

title: SAP Fiori — Content Density (Cozy and Compact) v1.96
key_claim: Cozy ≈ 3rem rows / 16px base; Compact ≈ 2rem rows / 14px base. Designed for user toggle.
relevance: Concrete numbers for our density toggle.
